ceiling_reason: Two core components (SPM gap-resolution, Mission Control polling) have zero source evidence in the repo. Auto-resolve logic and boardroom handoff are only partially evidenced. Testability is near-zero without a test plan. Score cannot reach 100 until all gaps are closed and every evidence row is SUPPORTED.

evidence.perspective: CEO-board liaison component referenced in proposal already exists as a file
Pre-build judge gate already exists; this proposal extends it
Ship gate pipeline component exists, relevant to gap-resolution flow
spec_pipeline is an established package; new components should slot in here

evidence.claim: A 'SPM gap-resolution' module exists in the repo
evidence.perspective: Proposal references SPM gap-resolution as a distinct component but no file evidence for it appears in the repo context

evidence.claim: Live Mission Control status polling mechanism exists
evidence.perspective: No Mission Control polling module or endpoint is evidenced in the provided repo context; WebSocket streaming exists but is not the same as a named Mission Control poller

evidence.claim: Judge gap auto-resolve logic exists or is scaffolded
evidence.perspective: prebuild_judge.py exists but proposal's 'auto-resolve' behaviour is not confirmed as implemented — only the file exists

evidence.claim: Machine spec pipeline is referenced in existing code
evidence.perspective: Multiple files reference 'machine' and 'spec pipeline' confirming the domain exists

evidence.claim: Tests exist for the spec pipeline / board components
evidence.perspective: Pipeline tests exist but no test file specifically covering judge gap-resolution or SPM gap-resolution is evidenced

evidence.claim: Boardroom integration point is defined and reachable
evidence.perspective: Proposal says gaps resolve 'before boardroom' but no boardroom route or agent file is cited in the repo context provided

gaps: SPM (Spec Pipeline Manager?) gap-resolution module is undefined — no file, no interface contract, no schema. Must be specified before build.
Mission Control polling: no endpoint URL, polling interval, auth mechanism, or failure-mode defined. Ambiguous whether this is a new service or reuse of existing WebSocket stream.
Auto-resolve logic for judge gaps is not specified: what constitutes a 'gap', what resolution strategies exist, what happens on unresolvable gaps (halt vs. escalate vs. default)?
No test plan provided for the new auto-resolve path — existing pipeline tests do not cover this flow.
'Boardroom' integration point is referenced but not defined in repo context; the handoff contract (data shape, trigger condition) is missing.
Security/auth for live Mission Control polling is unspecified — if polling is external-facing, auth must be declared.
Cost impact of live polling (frequency, compute, Railway/Vercel billing) is not estimated.
No rollback or circuit-breaker defined if CEO-board liaison or SPM resolution hangs mid-pipeline.
proposal: Machine spec pipeline: judge gaps auto-resolve via CEO-board liaison and SPM gap-resolution before boardroom, with live Mission Control status polling
